commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer label plus security-sensitive exec behavior and compatibility impact make this a human maintainer review path, not an automated repair lane.; Cleared: The diff tightens an exec approval security boundary and does not introduce a concrete supply-chain or secret-handling regression; compatibility risk is tracked separately. (sha=117f28f4fd5454c23193b5a8768c5cf813dd6c24)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes exec wrapper resolution and allowlist analysis so BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o/--output` wrappers are policy-blocked while plain stderr-only GNU `time` remains transparent.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +96. Total +178 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main marks `script` and `time` transparent and allowlist evaluation only fails once a segment is `policyBlocked`. I did not execute a current-main repro because this review was read-only, but the PR body also documents before/after direct allowlist probes.
commands.review_followup: Protected `maintainer` label plus security-sensitive exec behavior and compatibility impact make this a human maintainer review path, not an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: block side-effecting command wrappers [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat side-effecting dispatch wrapper forms as policy-blocked instead of transparent when evaluating exec approval allowlists.
- Keep plain GNU `time` usage transparent when it only reports to stderr, while blocking output-file forms before allowlist matching.
- Carry the requested analysis platform into command resolution so BSD/macOS wrapper behavior is tested deterministically from Linux.
- AI-assisted.

## Linked context

No public issue linked in this PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Exec approval allowlists no longer approve an inner command while preserving a side-effecting wrapper for execution.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with Node 24.15.0, repo Vitest wrapper, and direct OpenClaw exec-approval allowlist probes against the patched code.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with `evaluateShellAllowlist` probes for `script ~/.zshenv git log -1 --format='payload'` on `darwin`, `/usr/bin/time -o ~/.bashrc -a -f 'payload' git status` on `linux`, and `/usr/bin/time -p git status` on `linux`.
- Evidence after fix: `Test Files 3 passed (3); Tests 269 passed (269)`
- Evidence after fix: `bsd-script-darwin {"analysisOk":true,"allowlistSatisfied":false,"segmentSatisfiedBy":[null],"policyBlocked":true,"blockedWrapper":"script"}`
- Evidence after fix: `gnu-time-output-linux {"analysisOk":true,"allowlistSatisfied":false,"segmentSatisfiedBy":[null],"policyBlocked":true,"blockedWrapper":"time"}`
- Evidence after fix: `gnu-time-stderr-linux {"analysisOk":true,"allowlistSatisfied":true,"segmentSatisfiedBy":["allowlist"],"policyBlocked":false}`
- Observed result after fix: BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o` wrappers are policy-blocked before allowlist matching; plain `time -p` remains allowlist-transparent.
- What was not tested: Full suite and cross-OS runtime execution on macOS/FreeBSD were not run locally.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The BSD/macOS behavior is covered by platform-parameterized OpenClaw allowlist analysis in this Linux worktree rather than by running BSD `script` on a macOS/FreeBSD host.
- Before evidence: The previous behavior reduced wrapper commands to the inner command for allowlist matching while preserving the raw source command for execution.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`git diff --check`

`pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/infra/dispatch-wrapper-resolution.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allowlist.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.ts src/infra/exec-wrapper-trust-plan.ts src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-wrapper-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts`

`node --import tsx --input-type=module` direct `evaluateShellAllowlist` probes for the blocked `script` and `time -o` forms plus allowed `time -p`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Dispatch wrapper trust-plan tests for BSD/macOS `script` transcript wrappers and GNU `time -o` / `--output` wrappers.
- Command-resolution tests proving file-writing wrappers stay on the policy boundary.
- Allowlist tests proving the reported wrapper classes no longer satisfy an inner `git` allowlist while plain `time -p` still does.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The wrapper allowlist behavior could approve the reduced inner command while execution preserved the raw wrapper command.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Exec approval compatibility for users who intentionally wrapped allowlisted commands with transcript or output-file wrappers.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to wrapper policy transparency, preserves non-file-output `time` behavior, and adds focused regression coverage for blocked and still-transparent cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review gates and GitHub CI are pending.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer review and GitHub CI are pending; direct real-behavior proof has been added above.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper's real-behavior proof request was addressed by adding direct patched-code allowlist probe output to this PR body.
target.author: pgondhi987
